title: Scroll Equalizers, Reflowing Containers, Weight Ramps, Dough & @scope
description: Five browser-native drops: a scroll-timeline CSS equalizer, a self-reflowing container-query dashboard, scroll-entrance variable-font weight, liquid dough buttons, and @scope component boundaries.

drops.title: Scroll Equalizer
drops.description: Rows of CSS bars whose heights are driven by a single scroll-linked keyframe timeline, phase-shifted per bar via negative animation-delay for a chasing equalizer effect.
drops.concept: Uses animation-timeline: scroll(root) so bar height animates purely off document scroll position — no scroll event listeners at all. Each bar reuses the same @keyframes but starts at a different point in the timeline, producing a cascading wave as you scroll past three separate instances.

interaction.mouse: Scroll the page to drive the bars
interaction.click: Adjust bar count, stagger, and amplitude in the debug panel
interaction.touch: Scroll to drive the bars; debug panel hidden on mobile

drops.title: Resizable Container-Query Dashboard
drops.description: Drag any widget's corner and its internal content reflows — icon-over-text, then icon-beside-text, then a full stat block — based purely on the widget's own width.
drops.concept: Each widget is its own containment context (container-type: inline-size). @container queries inside swap the internal grid/flex layout at width thresholds that belong to the widget alone, independent of the viewport or sibling widgets — the textbook 'component that reflows itself' use case for container queries, made interactive with a real resize handle.
drops.source: LogRocket — "CSS in 2026: The new features reshaping frontend development"

interaction.mouse: Drag a widget's bottom-right handle to resize it
interaction.click: Toggle breakpoint badges / outline in the debug panel
interaction.touch: Drag the resize handle with a finger

drops.title: Weight Ramp
drops.description: Each word ramps its variable-font weight from thin to black the moment it scrolls into view, staggered word by word.
drops.concept: animation-timeline: view() ties a font-variation-settings keyframe animation to each word's own entry into the viewport (animation-range: entry). No IntersectionObserver, no scroll math — the browser drives the whole thing, and it reverses cleanly when you scroll back up.

interaction.mouse: Scroll to trigger each line's weight ramp
interaction.click: Tune start/end weight and stagger in the debug panel
interaction.touch: Scroll to trigger; debug panel hidden on mobile

drops.title: Dough Buttons
drops.description: Buttons stretch toward the cursor like dough being pulled, then spring back on release — the button's own outline deforms, no separate cursor blob involved.
drops.concept: Pointer position relative to each button's bounding box computes a per-vertex pull on a clip-path polygon, live, so the nearest edge bulges toward the cursor. Releasing the pointer transitions the polygon back to its resting hexagon with a spring easing curve.
drops.source: SpecificIT — "What Are the Trends in Button Design for 2026?"

interaction.mouse: Move the cursor near a button to pull its edge
interaction.click: Adjust pull strength / snap-back speed in the debug panel
interaction.touch: Tap triggers the resting state only (pull needs a hover-capable pointer)

drops.title: @scope Theme Cards
drops.description: Four cards share the exact same class names yet each resolves its own accent color, and a nested foreign widget stays untouched thanks to a donut-scoped CSS boundary.
drops.concept: @scope (.theme-card) to (.widget) limits where scoped selectors are allowed to match — the theme's .title/.btn rules apply inside each card but stop at the .widget boundary, so an embedded third-party-style component keeps its own plain look. Toggling the boundary off swaps in an unscoped version that visibly leaks the theme into the widget.
drops.source: Riad Kilani — "2026 CSS Features You Must Know"

interaction.mouse: Click the switch to toggle the scope boundary on/off
interaction.click: Toggle boundary; watch the nested widget gain/lose theming
